free_text: Measurement and hybrid model analysis of integral
excitation functions for alpha-particle reactions on
1332 keV, efficiency 16.5% relative to a 3" x 3"
NaI crystal at a distance of 25 cm).
Two types of stacks were applied in order to avoid
too large errors in the particle energy due to the
effect of energy straggling. The first one had to
degrade the alpha-energy from 172.5 MeV to 80 MeV
approximately and the second one from 90 MeV to
20 MeV approximately. Each stack consisted of a
number of 50 um Co foils alternately followed by
25 um Al catcher foils and by several commercial
grade Al degrader foils. In front of each stack
additionally a 100 um Al foil was placed for flux
The errors in absolute branching ratios are not
gamma-branching ratios have been considered in the
errors in those cases in which a sufficient number
of undisturbed gamma-lines could be used.
remarks of V. Mclane are included
